hero.subtitle: LAUNCHING 2026
hero.p1: Nasun's community will function as a creative studio, acquiring, developing, and managing a slate of narrative projects including films, animation series, and live-action shows. We will establish a transparent, consensus-driven process for greenlighting and shaping stories that the community is passionate about. This approach allows us to manage above-the-line talent effectively while preserving creative freedom across projects.
hero.p2: As founders, our lifelong passion lies at the intersection of cinema and technology. Naru, as head editor and producer, has contributed to seminal Korean films, while Overclocked has written and directed both sort and feature-length works, led two production and postproduction studios through the transition from film to digital, and was among the first to bring digital filmmaking into mainstream production in South Korea.
hero.p3: Having spent our lives immersed in all aspects of filmmaking, we believe there has never been a more exciting moment for storytellers, with professional-grade tools now widely accessible and transformative technologies like AI, Web3, and Unreal Engine 5 reshaping what's possible.
hero.p4: Our ambition for Rider Studio extends beyond creating exceptional films. We aim to pioneer a new era of global filmmaking, one that pushes the boundaries of story, aesthetics, and production while redefining how films are produced, marketed, and distributed. Ultimately, Rider Studio will serve as both a creative engine and an educational platform, showcasing the full professional process of filmmaking and inviting the community to learn, contribute, and innovate with us.

screenplay.title: Screenplay Competition
screenplay.description: A $100,000 prize competition for scripts suitable for South Korean film production with 5-10 million budget. Community voting through Script Round 1, 2, and 3 Roles to select the winning screenplay.

development.title: Development
development.description: Script revision with the screenwriter, hiring a Producer, and partnering with production companies. On-chain voting consensus required to greenlight the final script.

preProduction.title: Pre-Production / Production
preProduction.description: Community takes on a studio role monitoring progress. Access to set photos and daily updates. Director has final say on creative decisions. Community involvement only in emergency situations.

postProduction.title: Post-Production
postProduction.description: Editing, sound mix, color grading, and VFX. NDA-signed community members with Final Cut Role provide notes and approve the final mix-down.

marketing.title: Marketing / Distribution
marketing.description: Community collaborates on trailers, posters, and marketing materials. Working with professionals on traditional and creative distribution methods.

conceptToReality.title: Concept to Reality
conceptToReality.p1: This is an overview of Rider Studio producing films. In the bootstrap phase, the Rider Studio community will mean those who have bought and hold a Nasun Frontiers NFT. Film production in Web3 can exist where community involvement can add value to the complex process of producing commercial films for wide distribution. We invite not only professionals from both the creative and business sides of filmmaking to join us but also those in the community who want to get involved to learn how to develop a project from script to final mix-down and distribution.
conceptToReality.p2: The first films will be from Korean scripts which will be translated to English for the broader Rider Studio Community involvement. There are several key reasons for this: first, the core team has access to top talent in the Korean filmmaking industry; second, relative to the budget we can produce high quality films; third, having connections with streaming platforms and distributors operating in South Korea for global distribution; and finally but most importantly, Korean content has many precedents of becoming a global phenomenon. After establishing a track record, we can pursue producing content worldwide.

screenplayCompetition.title: Screenplay Competition $100,000
screenplayCompetition.p1: Rider will kick things off with an open screenplay competition with a cash prize. The main criteria for the scripts is that they be for a South Korean film production budget around 5-10 million. The core team and community members with the verified Script Round 1 Role will narrow down the script entries to a reasonable number agreed on by the community.
screenplayCompetition.p2: The core team and community members who read all the selected scripts will then get the Script Round 2 Role and gain permission to join a Discord channel where they can discuss the scripts. The rest of the community will only be able to read in that channel. Of course, we will encourage open discussions in the other public, general channels.
screenplayCompetition.p3: After ample time to read, discuss and reflect on the scripts, the members with the Script Round 2 Role will vote on-chain on the Nasun Network and narrow the scripts down to three.
screenplayCompetition.p4: The community members who want to get involved in the final selection process will have to read the last three scripts to get the Script Round 3 Role to join another dedicated channel and repeat the process outlined above. After another period of discussion and reflection, there will be a vote to select the winning script. The screen writer will then be given his prize money.

development.title: Development
development.p1: The community will then go through a process of revising the script with the screenwriter for a reasonable fee and amount of time. While the script is being worked on, we will discuss hiring a Producer, partnering with a production company or working with a studio. This conversation will be led by the core team to be honest and realistic without hype. After the community agrees on how to proceed informally without on-chain consensus, the core team will begin reaching out to producers, prodcos and studios with the script.
development.p2: Once a producer, whether by themselves or through an entity, joins the project, they will give an idea of how they see the film, and who they believe are good fits above the line, like the Director and the principal cast. The Producer will also give notes on the script. This will all be an informal discussion involving the community.
development.p3: The Producer will hire the Director, and they will together work with the screenwriter to finalize the script. This final script will need to get on-chain voting consensus by the community to get greenlit and move into production.

preProduction.title: Pre-Production / Production
preProduction.p1: Once the script is ready to go out, the community will take on more of a studio role where we let the Producer and Director do their work, and we monitor the rest of the development, preproduction and production stages. Of course we will have discussions about casting and hiring the rest of the crew, but this will mostly be informal. The Director will have final say in many of these creative decisions. We will have access to set photos and daily set reports. The community will not get involved during this time unless an emergency situation makes it necessary.

postProduction.title: Post-Production / Marketing / Distribution
postProduction.p1: Once shooting wraps, the community will once again get more involved now in postproduction and especially the ramping up of the marketing. We will work with the distributor and film marketing companies, producing trailers, posters and other materials. This will again be an informal discussion within the community to get ideas and figure out the best ways to proceed. The core team and select community members interested in this aspect of the process and have signed NDAs will directly interact with these companies.
postProduction.p2: While this is ongoing, there will have been the editing team that has been going through the dailies, selecting shots/takes and making a rough cut.The Director will decide when to present a cut to the community in a reasonable time frame. At this stage only select community members who have signed NDAs will be able to participate. The core team and the members with Final Cut Role will give notes to the Producer on the Director’s cut. The Director, depending on the agreed terms, will either work with the Editor to accommodate the given notes or be given an opportunity to pass on the notes. Again, depending on the agreement with the Director, the final cut may go through a consensus vote with the core team and members with Final Cut Role.
postProduction.p3: With the approved final cut, the film will go into sound mix, color grading and compositing special effects shots. While the Director and Producer coordinate these last stages in postproduction, the core team and the community will be working closely with the distributors' marketing for the release.
postProduction.p4: Once the sound mix, color grading and effects shots are done, the core team and the members with Final Cut Role will approve the final mix-down. With the film technically done(in the can), the community will continue being active in how to market and distribute, covering traditional methods as well as innovating new ways together as a community.

concluding.title: Concluding Thoughts
concluding.p1: On a personal note, as filmmakers who have always been passionate about the intersection of cinema and technology, we are excited to see where we can take film in the coming years. As students of the history of cinema, we have been involved in all aspects of filmmaking for decades, and we believe today is the most exciting time with the advent of these new technologies.
concluding.p2: We believe in a short time we can be producing a new era of global films that are not only pushing the boundaries of story and aesthetics but also pioneering new modes of development, production, marketing and distribution. One of our biggest aspirations for Rider Studio besides making great films is to teach others the complete professional process of filmmaking so they can then teach us where we can take it in the future.

riderStudio.title: Concept to Reality
riderStudio.description1: This is a conceptual approach to how Rider Studio can produce films with the Nasun community that will be open for discussions in the coming months. By Nasun community, we currently mean those who have bought and hold a Frontiers NFT. This is a realistic approach to how film production in Web3 can exist where community involvement is only considered if it can add value to the complex process of professional filmmaking to produce commercial films for wide distribution. This will also be a way for those in the community who want to get involved to learn how to develop a project from script to final mix-down and distribution.
riderStudio.description2: The first films will be from Korean scripts which will be translated to English for the broader Nasun Community involvement. There are several reasons the first films will be Korean: the first is that the core team has access to the best talent in the Korean filmmaking industry; the second is that relative to the budget we can produce very high quality films; the third is having connections with streaming platforms and distributors operating in South Korea we will have a much better chance of being profitable; and finally but most importantly, Korean content if done right has many precedents of becoming a global hit. After establishing a track record and some buzz, we can pursue producing films in the U.S.

screenplay.title: Screenplay Competition $250,000
screenplay.description1: Rider will kick things off with an open screenplay competition with a $250,000 prize. The main criteria for the scripts is that they be for a South Korean film production budget around 5-10 million. The core team and community members with the verified Script Round 1 Role selected from holders of the Frontiers NFTs will narrow down the script entries to a reasonable number agreed on by the community.
screenplay.description2: The core team and community members who read all the selected scripts will then get the Script Round 2 Role and gain permission to join a Discord channel where they can post to discuss the scripts. The rest of the community will only be able to read in that channel. Of course, we will encourage open discussions in the other public, general channels.
screenplay.description3: After ample time to read, discuss and reflect on the scripts, the members with the Script Round 2 Role will vote on-chain on the Nasun Network and narrow the scripts down to three.
screenplay.description4: The Nasun community who want to get involved in the final selection process will have to read the last three scripts to get the Script Round 3 Role to join another dedicated channel and repeat the process outlined above. After another period of discussion and reflection, there will be a vote to select the winning script. The screen writer will then be given his prize money of $250,000.

description1: Core team and selected community members receive the 'Script Round 2 Role'
Gain access to a Discord channel to discuss the scripts
description2: Spend time reading, reflecting, and discussing the scripts
Members with the role vote on-chain via the Nasun Network
description3: Community members read the final three scripts to earn 'Script Round 3 Role'
Join a new Discord channel to repeat the discussion and selection process

While the script is being worked on, we will discuss hiring a Producer, partnering with a production company or working with a studio. This conversation will be led by the core team to be honest and realistic without hype. After the community agrees on how to proceed informally without on-chain consensus, the core team will begin reaching out to producers, prodcos and studios with the script.

The Producer will also give notes on the script. This will all be an informal discussion involving the community.
development.description3: The Producer will hire the Director, and they will together work with the screenwriter to finalize the script. This final script will need to get on-chain voting consensus by the Nasun community to get greenlit and move into production.

preProduction.title: Pre-Production / Production

Of course we will have discussions with casting and hiring the rest of the crew but this will mostly be informal and the Director will have final say in many of these creative decisions. We will have access to set photos and daily set photos. Nasun Community will not get involved during this time unless an emergency situation makes it necessary.

postProduction.title: Post-Production / Marketing / Distribution
postProduction.description1: Once shooting wraps, the community will once again participate more now in postproduction and especially the ramping up of the marketing. We will work with film marketing companies, producing trailers, posters and other materials. This will again be an informal discussion within the community to get ideas and figure out the best ways to proceed. The core team will directly interact with these companies, but after a few iterations, we could have interested community members join in.
postProduction.description2: While this is ongoing, there will have been the editing team that has been going through the dailies, selecting shots/takes and making a rough cut.The Director will decide when to present a cut to the community in a reasonable time frame. At this stage only select community members who have signed NDAs will be able to participate. The core team and the members with Final Cut Role will give notes to the Producer on the Director’s cut. The Director, depending on the agreed terms, will either work with the Editor to accommodate the given notes or be given an opportunity to pass on the notes. Again, depending on the agreement with the Director, the final cut may go through a consensus vote with the core team and members with Final Cut Role.
postProduction.description3: With the approved final cut, the film will go into sound mix, color grading and compositing special effects shots. While the Director and Producer coordinates these last stages in postproduction, the core team and  Nasun community will be working closely with marketing teams and start collaborating with the distribution entities.
postProduction.description4: Once the sound mix, color grading and effects shots are done, the core team and the members with Final Cut Role will approve the final mix-down. With the film technically done(in the can), the Nasun community will continue being active in how to market and distribute working with professionals covering traditional methods as well as coming up with new creative, effective ways together as a community.

conclusion.title: Concluding Thoughts
conclusion.description1: On a personal note, as someone who has always been passionate about the intersection of cinema and technology, I am excited to see where we can take film in the coming years. I have led two production/postproduction houses upgrade from film/tape format to digital as well as be one of the first to bring digital filmmaking to South Korea. As a student of the history of cinema, I have been involved in all aspects of filmmaking throughout my life, and I believe today is the most exciting time with the advent of these technologies like AI, Web3, UE and also the accessibility of professional hardware equipment.
conclusion.description2: I believe in a short time we can be producing a new era of global, blockbuster films that are not only pushing the boundaries of story and aesthetics but also pioneering new modes of development, production, marketing and distribution. One of my biggest aspirations for this project besides making great films is to teach others the complete professional process of filmmaking so they can then teach me where we can take it in the future.
